Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi N41 Bus Timetable Corniche Hospital to ADNEC

June 21, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus N41 (overnight) is one of its key routes, running between Corniche Hospital and ADNEC (Abu Dhabi National Exhibition Centre) and providing an important late-night connection across the city. In this guide, you are going to get a complete review of the N41 route, its operational time, stops, and tips for traveling on this route. As a resident or visitor, the N41 guarantees an easy journey throughout the night.

Contents

Operational Details

Service N41 of the bus also runs overnight, an important service for night travelers. Below is the detailed schedule for Bus N41, departing from both Corniche Hospital and ADNEC:

From Corniche Hospital From ADNEC
00:32 00:53
01:32 01:53
02:32 02:56
03:32 03:55
04:32 04:52

Bus N41 TimetableFor the full schedule, visit the official AD Mobility website or follow this link for the N41 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing: Hafilat Card is a convenient cashless payment system by Abu Dhabi for paying for Bus N41. Journeys within one zone cost AED 2, with an extra charge of AED 0.05 per kilometer beyond that first zone. It is very easy to top up your Hafilat card online or in any of the kiosks spread all over the city and enjoy a smooth travel experience.

Route Overview

N41 bus route is connecting the important locations in Abu Dhabi and covers the residential as well as commercial areas. Below is an overview of the main stops along the route:

  1. Corniche Hospital: The starting point for passengers heading towards ADNEC, a prominent exhibition and conference center.

  2. Al Mina Street: A lively area filled with shopping, dining, and entertainment options.

  3. Hamdan Street: A central business and commercial district known for its retail outlets and business establishments.

  4. Hazaa Bin Zayed Mosque: A significant religious site in Abu Dhabi.

  5. Sheikh Zayed Bin Sultan Street: One of the main roads in Abu Dhabi, connecting several important areas of the city.

  6. Emirates Tower: A major office building located in the business district.

  7. Al Mariah Mall: A popular shopping mall offering a wide range of retail stores and services.

  8. Ministry of Economy: The central government office responsible for economic policies and regulations.

  9. Sultan Bin Zayed The First Street: A busy street lined with commercial and residential buildings.

  10. Liwa Center: A shopping and commercial center offering various services and retail outlets.

  11. Sheikh Zayed 1st Mosque: A key spiritual and cultural landmark in the city.

  12. Etisalat: A stop near the headquarters of the telecommunications company.

  13. Al Falah Street: A road providing access to nearby neighborhoods and residential areas.

  14. Al Tabbiyah Mosque: A mosque located along the route, offering a place for prayer and reflection.

  15. SKMC (Sheikh Khalifa Medical City): A major healthcare facility offering a wide range of medical services.

  16. Omar Bin Al Khatab Mosque: A well-known mosque that is an important place of worship.

  17. Abu Dhabi Food Control Authority: A key stop for food-related services and regulations in the city.

  18. Delma Street: A major street offering commercial and retail services.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. ITC Bus Depot: A transportation hub serving as a central point for various bus routes.

  2. Mohammed Bin Khalifa Street: A well-known street that hosts numerous offices and businesses.

  3. Ministry of Information & Culture: A government office responsible for the country’s information, media, and culture policies.

  4. Al Jazeera Sports Club: A local sports venue offering various recreational and fitness activities.

  5. Immigration Department: A vital government building for immigration services and administration.

  6. Khalifa University: A prestigious educational institution offering a range of academic programs.

25.

  1. Al Nahda National School: An educational institution catering to local students.

  2. Salama Bint Butti Street: A road providing access to surrounding residential areas.

  3. Al Ghaf Park: A public park offering green spaces for relaxation and leisure activities.

  4. Mishrif Mall: A shopping and entertainment destination popular among locals.

  5. Al Dhafra Street: A major thoroughfare providing access to various businesses and services.

  6. Mawgab Street: A street that combines both residential and commercial areas.

  7. Ministry of Foreign Trade: A significant government office focusing on international trade policies and agreements.

  8. Wakr Street: A connecting road that links various local neighborhoods within the city.

  9. Zayed Military Hospital: A prominent healthcare facility providing medical services to military personnel and the public.

  10. ADNEC: The final stop, located at the Abu Dhabi National Exhibition Centre, a key venue for exhibitions, conferences, and events.

Some other bus routes in Abu Dhabi you can take are the N2 overnight bus route from Ghurair Al Ojaan Mosque to Abu Dhabi Airport ECC.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Passengers on Bus N41 can conveniently access several notable attractions in Abu Dhabi:

  1. Emirates Tower: A key business landmark in the city.

  2. Al Mariah Mall: A popular destination for shopping and dining.

3.

  1. ADNEC: The exhibition center hosts numerous international events, offering great opportunities for business and leisure visitors.

Journey Experience

The N41 overnight bus from Corniche Hospital to ADNEC provided a smooth and scenic trip through Abu Dhabi in recent times. Passengers on the long ride had a rare sight of the city at night passing through lively areas such as Hamdan Street and Emirates Tower, and witnessing a mix of modern skyscrapers and cultural landmarks.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: June 21, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
